content:
@Gary Guo -- here's the issue I'm running into using arbitrary_self_types: https://play.rust-lang.org/?version=nightly&mode=debug&edition=2018&gist=6bd3444cfaf1664d08433ec5b769f572

I get the same error if I use receiver_trait: https://play.rust-lang.org/?version=nightly&mode=debug&edition=2018&gist=15fbe7b378f7128807fd6dc7006eaefc

(Note that if I use Arc<Self> in TestTrait it works.)

Do you know where int he compiler's source code it decides which types can be used as self types but still allow &dyn on the trait?

You'll need to use CoerceUnsized and DispatchFromDyn in order for the pointer type to support dynamic dispatch: https://play.rust-lang.org/?version=nightly&mode=debug&edition=2018&gist=b1e7c2183fd2ee4f1970b19864d6ed6b

Thanks Gary! I'll update the Ref PR to behave as you suggested, it will be very similar to Arc<T>, i.e., it wraps T and requires &Ref<T> to increment the refcount (no unsafe involved), but it is backed by refcount_t, has no weak references, and has no get_mut()
